title: Niedenzuella peruviana
intro: Niedenzuella peruviana, also known as Peruvian Niedenzuella, is a plant species in the family Melastomataceae, native to the tropical regions of Peru. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including skin infections and respiratory issues. Niedenzuella peruviana contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Niedenzuella peruviana include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.

The family Melastomataceae is a large and diverse group of plants that are commonly known as the melastome or memecyclid families.
